_note: Default config the `setting-up-rag` skill applies to a doc set. Local-first: Qdrant + FastEmbed (CPU, no cloud). Heading-aware chunking with a min-merge floor (avoids the tiny-section chunk explosion), hybrid dense+sparse retrieval fused with RRF, then a local cross-encoder rerank. Validate and adjust against a held-out gold set per TUNING.md before trusting a change; never keep a knob that does not beat the baseline.

embedding._alt_dense: BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5 (768d, higher quality, slower) — re-index if changed
embedding._alt_sparse: prithivida/Splade_PP_en_v1 (learned sparse; better recall, larger/slower than bm25)

chunker._note: size/overlap/min_words in WORDS. min_words merges tiny heading sections so a heading-dense doc does not explode into hundreds of one-line chunks. code_* apply when --kind code.

hybrid._note: Each arm (dense, sparse) fetches `prefetch` candidates; RRF fuses their rank lists. DBSF is the score-based alternative.

rerank._note: Cross-encoder re-scores the top_n fused candidates in-process, then top_k are returned. Disable for the lowest-latency/plain arm.

answering._note: Testing-only answer generation on top of retrieval. Calls an OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions endpoint such as local vLLM, TensorRT-LLM, or a cloud provider. This is not part of the default retrieval workflow.
answering.system_prompt: You are a retrieval-grounded QA test harness. Answer only from the provided context. Cite supporting chunks with bracketed source ids like [1]. If the context is insufficient, say what is missing instead of guessing.
